questions.question: Solve |x| = 7.
questions.explanation: The absolute value of x equals 7 when x is 7 units from zero on the number line. This happens at both x = 7 and x = -7.

questions.question: Solve |x - 3| = 5.
questions.explanation: The expression (x - 3) must equal 5 or -5. If x - 3 = 5, then x = 8. If x - 3 = -5, then x = -2.

questions.question: Which value(s) of x satisfy |x + 2| = 0?
questions.explanation: Absolute value equals zero only when the expression inside equals zero. So x + 2 = 0, giving x = -2.

questions.question: Solve |2x| = 12.
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 2: |x| = 6. Then x = 6 or x = -6.

questions.question: A temperature reading has an absolute error of |t - 68| = 3, where t is the actual temperature in degrees Fahrenheit. What are the possible temperatures?
questions.explanation: The actual temperature is 3 degrees away from 68. So t - 68 = 3 (giving t = 71) or t - 68 = -3 (giving t = 65).

questions.question: Solve |x| = -4.
questions.explanation: Absolute value is always non-negative, so |x| can never equal -4. There is no solution.
